content.subject: Refactor types and constants for segment/zoom-target/step model
content.description: Add SegmentConfig, ZoomTarget, PresentationStep (union: camera/advance/context-zoom), StageController interfaces. Deprecate CanvasElementConfig and old WalkthroughStep. Add segment-dimension constants (base width/height, ribbon gap).

content.subject: Remove focus overlay; add stage-controller registry
content.description: Delete src/lib/focus/ directory (FocusOverlay.svelte, focus.svelte.ts). Remove FocusOverlay from App.svelte. Create a stage-controller registry so segments can expose advance/retreat hooks to the walkthrough engine.
content.activeForm: Removing focus overlay and adding stage registry

content.subject: Refactor canvas to segment ribbon with camera helpers
content.description: Replace CanvasLayer/CanvasElement with SegmentLayer/Segment wrapper. Auto-lay-out segments left-to-right with a gap. Add helpers to compute camera target from segment landing or zoom target, factoring in viewport size. Wire optional bounded vertical scroll within a segment post-staging.

content.subject: Refactor walkthrough engine and keyboard handler to unified flat script
content.description: Walkthrough step is now a union: camera (go to segment landing or zoom target), advance (call segment stage controller), or context-zoom (fit adjacent segments). One next()/prev() dispatches on type. KeyboardHandler: space or right-arrow advances, left-arrow retreats; drop focus check.
content.activeForm: Refactoring walkthrough engine and keyboard handler

content.subject: Update global styles: Inter font, gradient headline, green accent
content.description: Import Inter via Google Fonts. Set body font to Inter. Add CSS variables for headline gradient stops, human-green accent, code block styling. Remove old generic accent colors.

content.subject: Build Agentic Chat Segment component (all 21 stages)
content.description: Implement AgenticChatSegment.svelte per content_briefs.md: pre-staged chat with user bubbles, AI replies, collapsible thinking section, tool-use cards, inline code blocks, search-result excerpts. 21 stages total across 4 parts. Own stage controller, auto-scroll-to-newest via camera.y animation. Zoom targets: Python tool-call card, error-state code block, source excerpt, thinking section.

content.subject: Author segments.config and walkthrough.config for chat segment
content.description: Replace slides.config.ts with segments.config.ts (just the chat segment for now, plus its zoom targets). Rewrite walkthrough.config.ts as a flat PresentationStep[] covering: land on segment, 21 advance steps, zoom-to-python-card, zoom-to-error-code, zoom-to-source-excerpt, back to overview.

content.subject: Verify build, run svelte-check, and dev-server smoke test
content.description: Run svelte-check to catch type errors. Run npm run build. Start dev server and confirm no runtime errors in the initial render.

content.text:
Navigation overhaul and first segment are in. Summary of what landed:

**Navigation system (Decisions #5-#10)**
- New types: `SegmentConfig`, `ZoomTarget`, `PresentationStep` union (camera / advance / context-zoom), `StageController` with optional runtime `getZoomTargetRect`.
- `src/lib/segments/` with `segments.svelte.ts` (ribbon layout, auto xOffsets), `SegmentLayer.svelte`, and `camera-targets.ts` (segment landing, zoom-to-target, context-zoom helpers).
- `src/lib/stage/stage-registry.ts` so segments expose advance/retreat to the engine.
- Rewritten `walkthrough.svelte.ts` - flat step list, unified next/prev, camera-state history for undo, `activeSegmentId` tracking.
- `KeyboardHandler`: space/arrows drive advance-or-camera uniformly; no more focus gating.
- `FocusOverlay` and old `CanvasLayer`/`CanvasElement` deleted.
- `camera.svelte.ts` gained `scrollTo(y)` so auto-scroll and camera moves share the same tween slot.

**Visual identity**
- `app.css`: Inter + JetBrains Mono, green-human + vibrant AI gradient tokens, headline `.headline-gradient` utility, dark-surface palette.
- `index.html` font preload updated.

**Agentic Chat Segment (all 21 stages, per brief)**
- `src/content/segments/chat/AgenticChatSegment.svelte` with companion `stage.svelte.ts`.
- Staged reveal of user/AI bubbles, struck-through wrong answer, two thinking sections, three Python tool-call cards (error, fix, validation), web-search card, source excerpt, cited final answer.
- Seven runtime-measured zoom targets: `thinking-one/two`, `python-run/fix/validation`, `search-results`, `source-excerpt`.
- Auto-scrolls camera so the newest staged element anchors ~85% down the viewport; post-staging the camera is free to be driven elsewhere by the script.
- `walkthrough.config.ts` scripts 29 steps: land, 21 advances interleaved with 4 zoom-ins and a final zoom-out.

**Verified**
- `svelte-check`: 0 errors / 0 warnings.
- `npm run build`: clean production build.
- `npm run dev`: server starts, serves pages, compiles modules.
- **Not** verified: visual rendering, zoom target framing, auto-scroll feel, font loading. Run `npm run dev` and open http://localhost:5173; arrows or space to advance, arrow-left to step back.

Expect some tuning needed on first view - zoom-target framing and the auto-scroll anchor point are common places to iterate once you see it in the browser.

content: We overhauled the presentation navigation (left-to-right ribbon, discrete zoom targets, seamless interactive embedding, flat script) and built the 21-stage Agentic Chat segment; build and svelte-check both pass. Next: run `npm run dev` and visually verify, then tune zoom-target framing and auto-scroll feel. (disable recaps in /config)

content.text: I can't access Chrome DevTools, but the stack trace is diagnostic enough. The bug is in `App.svelte`: the two `load()` calls are inside a `$effect`. `walkthrough.load` reads `segments.placed` (reactive) while `segments.load` writes it, so the effect re-runs itself in a loop. Initialization like this should run once at script setup, not in an effect.

<script lang="ts">
  import './app.css';
  import BackgroundCanvas from '$lib/background/BackgroundCanvas.svelte';
  import CameraViewport from '$lib/camera/CameraViewport.svelte';
  import SegmentLayer from '$lib/segments/SegmentLayer.svelte';
  import WalkthroughControls from '$lib/walkthrough/WalkthroughControls.svelte';
  import KeyboardHandler from '$lib/ui/KeyboardHandler.svelte';
  import { segments } from '$lib/segments/segments.svelte';
  import { walkthrough } from '$lib/walkthrough/walkthrough.svelte';
  import { segmentConfigs } from '$content/segments.config';
  import { presentationConfig } from '$content/walkthrough.config';

  $effect(() => {
    segments.load(segmentConfigs);
    walkthrough.load(presentationConfig.steps);
  });
</script>

<BackgroundCanvas />

<CameraViewport>
  <SegmentLayer placed={segments.placed} />
</CameraViewport>

<WalkthroughControls />
<KeyboardHandler />

content.text:
The `$effect` block was re-running in a loop because `walkthrough.load` reads `segments.placed` while `segments.load` writes it. Both `load()` calls are static initialization and now run once at script setup. Also added an empty `favicon.ico` link to silence the 404.

HMR picked up both changes, so the running dev server should already reflect them - the infinite-loop error should be gone on next page load.

Quick scan of remaining `$effect` blocks for the same anti-pattern came back clean: `AgenticChatSegment`'s effects only read `chatStage.stageIndex` synchronously (writes happen in `tick().then(...)`, which is untracked), and `SegmentLayer`'s dynamic-import effect is idempotent because `loadComponent` early-returns once loaded. Let me know if the browser console still complains or surfaces anything else.

message.content: Ok, works. A few stylistic tweaks: the headline with the gradient gets clipped at the bottom (visible on the 'g'). I am not sure if I like the green backdrop of the user chat bubbles what would be a better color - iot's probably from whatsapp - any alternatives?

content.text: Two fixes. Headline clipping is the too-tight `line-height: 1.02` - descenders like 'g' drop below the background-clip box. The WhatsApp association is just the green fill - swap it for a neutral raised surface and let green show up elsewhere (avatar/accent) to keep the human semantic without the mint-bubble look.

content.text:
Applied: headline line-height 1.02 → 1.12 plus a tiny `padding-bottom` to give descenders room; bubble is now a neutral translucent panel (`rgba(255,255,255,0.05)`) with a matching subtle border.

If the neutral feels too quiet, three alternatives you can swap by changing just the `.bubble` background/border pair:

- **Cool slate** - `rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.08)` / `rgba(148, 163, 184, 0.2)`. Reads Linear/Cursor-ish.
- **Warm amber wash** - `rgba(251, 191, 36, 0.06)` / `rgba(251, 191, 36, 0.18)`. Subtle tint that picks up the headline gradient's tail.
- **Green as accent only** - neutral bg plus a 3px green left border (`border-left: 3px solid var(--color-human)`), which keeps the human semantic without the fill.

Green is still the human color in the palette - it'll show up more prominently on the sessions-flow segment where it's carrying data meaning.

import type { PresentationConfig } from '$lib/types';

const SEGMENT = 'agentic-chat';

export const presentationConfig: PresentationConfig = {
  name: 'AI for Interactive Media',
  description: 'Session 6 presentation deck - canvas-based spatial walkthrough.',
  steps: [
    // Land on the segment (near-instant; load() already anchors the camera).
    {
      id: 'land',
      type: 'camera',
      segmentId: SEGMENT,
      duration: 0.01,
      label: 'From chatbot to agent',
    },

content.text:
Applied. Summary of the change:

- New `spotlight` step type. It animates only `camera.y` (keeps zoom and x), anchoring the target at ~40% from the top of the viewport via a new `scrollYForTarget` helper.
- The chat segment derives a `highlightedId` from `walkthrough.currentStep` - when the current step is a spotlight on this segment, the matching card gets a `.spotlighted` class.
- Spotlighted cards get a soft purple/pink outer glow and grow a gradient "ribbon" across their top edge with a wrench icon and `TOOL USE · python` (or `web_search` / etc.) - inline with `overflow: hidden`, no layout drama.
- Walkthrough config: all four tool-card zoom-ins (`python-run`, `python-validation`, `search-results`, `source-excerpt`) switched to `spotlight`. The two "back to overview" steps stay as camera steps and automatically clear the highlight (derived from `currentStep`, so as soon as the current step isn't a spotlight, the ribbon + glow disappear and the normal flow resumes).
- `svelte-check` clean; HMR should have picked it up on the running dev server.

The ribbon/glow already clears automatically when `currentStep` stops being a spotlight, so this extra back-to-overview step is redundant. Removing it.

1. (stage 4) **A chatbot answers from intuition - and can get simple things wrong.**

3. (stage 11) **Tools let the model validate its own work before replying.**

5. (stage 18) **Training data has a cut-off; live tools extend the reach.**
6. (stage 21) **Answers come cited - the model shows its sources.**

Wiring up the rails now. Both live inside the segment (world-space) with `top` driven by `camera.y` so they track the viewport as it scrolls.

content.text:
Rails added, type-check clean. Summary:

- **Right rail** (`takeaways`): 6 bullets, each a `reveal(stage)` gate - appears with a small fade + slide-in when the corresponding staged content lands. Headed by a `TAKEAWAYS` monospace label and a thin divider. All numbered `01-06`. Positioned at x=1448 within the segment (right side, 400px wide).
- **Left rail**: smaller gradient headline (`lr-headline`) + a short caption. Hidden by default (`opacity: 0`); fades in once `viewportTopY > HEADLINE_BOTTOM - 40`, i.e. when the main hero has scrolled out. Positioned at x=72.
- Both rails use `transform: translate(x, railTopY)` where `railTopY` is derived from `camera.y` so they track the viewport as the camera scrolls through the tall segment. They stay pinned ~120px below the viewport top.
- Both live inside the segment, so when the camera moves to a future segment they're naturally off-screen; no global reset needed.

Bullets are the first draft - fire back any edits on wording or stage pairings and I'll tune.

message.content: NOpe not working. If I move to step 7, there is not scrolling into view, and I just about see the "Thinking" indicator of the next bubble.

content.text:
Found it. The `transform: translateX(-300px)` on `.chat-column` turns chat-column into a containing block, which makes it the `offsetParent` for its staged children. So `last.offsetTop` is measured relative to chat-column (not chat-root), losing the ~650px of header/padding above it - which pushes the computed target below `minY` and the camera gets clamped to the segment top.

Fix: walk the `offsetParent` chain up to `rootEl` and sum. Same for the zoom-target measurements.

file.content:
# CLAUDE.md

## Project Conventions

- **Documentation**: Keep all project docs in [`docs/`](docs/) (`project_info.md`, `technical_info.md`, `design_guide.md`, `key_decisions.md`, `key_learnings.md`) up to date with every significant change.
- **Session logging**: Use `/logsession` at the end of each session to append contributions to [`docs/session_log.md`](docs/session_log.md). This is the single source of truth for the contribution log. Can also be run retrospectively from audit files.

## Writing Style

- **No long dashes**: Never use em-dashes (`—`) or en-dashes (`–`) in any presented content (ads, emails, documentation copy, slide text, etc.). Use hyphens (`-`), commas, or rephrase.

## Git Use

- **Commit tagging**: Claude's commits include a `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>` trailer. Human commits won't have this trailer. Detailed per-contribution attribution lives in `docs/session_log.md`.
- **Commit frequency**: Commit frequently in meaningful batches after changes. Commit updates to the session_audits separately, but only commit sessions that have been concluded - avoid tracking sessions as they are ongoing, this history is not meaningful.

content.text: Now updating CLAUDE.md with the commit-frequency guidance.

input.old_string: - **Commit frequency**: Commit frequently in meaningful batches after changes. Commit updates to the session_audits separately, but only commit sessions that have been concluded - avoid tracking sessions as they are ongoing, this history is not meaningful.
input.new_string: - **Commit frequency**: Commit frequently, in small, meaningful batches as work is completed - ideally after each focused change (a new segment, a bug fix, a design pass). Do not let many unrelated changes pile up in a single commit; smaller, well-scoped commits make file-level progress easier to track and revert. Commit updates to the session_audits separately, but only commit sessions that have been concluded - avoid tracking sessions as they are ongoing, this history is not meaningful.

toolUseResult.originalFile:
# CLAUDE.md

## Project Conventions

- **Documentation**: Keep all project docs in [`docs/`](docs/) (`project_info.md`, `technical_info.md`, `design_guide.md`, `key_decisions.md`, `key_learnings.md`) up to date with every significant change.
- **Session logging**: Use `/logsession` at the end of each session to append contributions to [`docs/session_log.md`](docs/session_log.md). This is the single source of truth for the contribution log. Can also be run retrospectively from audit files.

## Writing Style

- **No long dashes**: Never use em-dashes (`—`) or en-dashes (`–`) in any presented content (ads, emails, documentation copy, slide text, etc.). Use hyphens (`-`), commas, or rephrase.

## Git Use

- **Commit tagging**: Claude's commits include a `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>` trailer. Human commits won't have this trailer. Detailed per-contribution attribution lives in `docs/session_log.md`.
- **Commit frequency**: Commit frequently in meaningful batches after changes. Commit updates to the session_audits separately, but only commit sessions that have been concluded - avoid tracking sessions as they are ongoing, this history is not meaningful.

lines: - **Commit tagging**: Claude's commits include a `Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>` trailer. Human commits won't have this trailer. Detailed per-contribution attribution lives in `docs/session_log.md`.

-- **Commit frequency**: Commit frequently in meaningful batches after changes. Commit updates to the session_audits separately, but only commit sessions that have been concluded - avoid tracking sessions as they are ongoing, this history is not meaningful.

+- **Commit frequency**: Commit frequently, in small, meaningful batches as work is completed - ideally after each focused change (a new segment, a bug fix, a design pass). Do not let many unrelated changes pile up in a single commit; smaller, well-scoped commits make file-level progress easier to track and revert. Commit updates to the session_audits separately, but only commit sessions that have been concluded - avoid tracking sessions as they are ongoing, this history is not meaningful.

Design pass: left-to-right ribbon, zoom targets, segment content briefs

Tightens the presentation design language based on a design session: segments
flow left-to-right with vertical per-segment expansion, discrete authored zoom
targets replace free zoom, interactive content embeds seamlessly (no overlay
chrome), navigation unifies behind a flat presentation script, and inter-segment
context-zoom becomes an optional transition. Typography pinned to Inter Semi
Bold with gradient headlines; human = green, AI = vibrant gradient. Performance
deferred.

- CLAUDE.md: tighter commit-frequency guidance; frequent small batches
- docs/design_guide.md: rewritten around the new visual + navigation rules
- docs/key_decisions.md: adds #5-#10 for ribbon, zoom targets, seamless focus,
  unified script, context-zoom, and deferred perf; #1 refined by #5
- docs/project_info.md: updated core concepts
- docs/technical_info.md: pending-revisions section mapping decisions to code
- docs/content_briefs.md: briefs for agentic-chat and sessions-flow segments

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)" && git status --short | head -5

Rebuild navigation around segments; add skills and agentic-chat segments

Replaces the old canvas element + focus overlay architecture with a segment

stage advances; new spotlight step type scrolls to a zoom target and highlights
it without changing zoom. Seamless interactive embedding: no overlay, no chrome.

Core library:

  (camera | advance | context-zoom | spotlight)

  (cameraTargetForSegment, cameraTargetForZoom, cameraTargetForContextZoom,
  scrollYForTarget)
- stage/: per-segment stage-controller registry
- walkthrough: unified engine with camera-state history for prev(), dispatches

import type { PresentationConfig } from '$lib/types';

const SKILLS = 'skills';
const SEGMENT = 'agentic-chat';

export const presentationConfig: PresentationConfig = {
  name: 'AI for Interactive Media',
  description: 'Session 6 presentation deck - canvas-based spatial walkthrough.',
  steps: [
    // --- Segment 1: Skills landscape ---
    {
      id: 'land-skills',
      type: 'camera',
      segmentId: SKILLS,
      duration: 0.01,
      label: 'Skills landscape',
    },
    { id: 'k01', type: 'advance', segmentId: SKILLS, label: 'Programming' },
    { id: 'k02', type: 'advance', segmentId: SKILLS, label: 'UI & frontend' },
    { id: 'k03', type: 'advance', segmentId: SKILLS, label: 'Visual design' },
    { id: 'k04', type: 'advance', segmentId: SKILLS, label: 'Interaction design' },

    {
      id: 'to-chat',
      type: 'camera',
      segmentId: SEGMENT,
      duration: 1.4,

    // Part 1 - chatbot baseline (4 advances)
    { id: 'a01', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'User: casual hello' },

    { id: 'a03', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'User: banana question' },

    // Part 2 - agentic mode (9 advances)
    { id: 'a05', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'Revise - work it out' },
    { id: 'a06', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'Thinking opens' },
    { id: 'a07', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'Thinking: case-insensitive' },
    { id: 'a08', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'Python: first attempt' },
    { id: 'a09', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'Python: NameError' },
    { id: 'a10', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'Python: fixed code' },
    { id: 'a11', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'Python: validation asserts' },
    { id: 'a12', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'Result: 3' },
    { id: 'a13', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'AI: corrected answer' },

    // Part 3 - narration: scroll + highlight the two python tool-use cards
    {
      id: 'z15',
      type: 'spotlight',
      segmentId: SEGMENT,
      targetId: 'python-run',
      duration: 0.9,
      label: 'Spotlight: python tool-call (error)',
    },
    {
      id: 'z16',
      type: 'spotlight',
      segmentId: SEGMENT,
      targetId: 'python-validation',
      duration: 0.9,
      label: 'Spotlight: validation asserts',
    },

    // Part 4 - live-data demo; spotlights instead of zoom-ins for consistency
    { id: 'a16', type: 'advance', segmentId: SEGMENT, label: 'User: 2026 shipments?' },

<script lang="ts">
  import './app.css';
  import BackgroundCanvas from '$lib/background/BackgroundCanvas.svelte';
  import CameraViewport from '$lib/camera/CameraViewport.svelte';
  import SegmentLayer from '$lib/segments/SegmentLayer.svelte';
  import WalkthroughControls from '$lib/walkthrough/WalkthroughControls.svelte';
  import KeyboardHandler from '$lib/ui/KeyboardHandler.svelte';
  import { segments } from '$lib/segments/segments.svelte';
  import { walkthrough } from '$lib/walkthrough/walkthrough.svelte';
  import { segmentConfigs } from '$content/segments.config';
  import { presentationConfig } from '$content/walkthrough.config';

  // One-time initialization: static configs; must NOT run inside $effect,
  // because walkthrough.load reads segments.placed which segments.load writes,
  // which would put the effect in a self-triggering loop.
  segments.load(segmentConfigs);
  walkthrough.load(presentationConfig.steps);
</script>

<BackgroundCanvas />

<CameraViewport>
  <SegmentLayer placed={segments.placed} />
</CameraViewport>

<WalkthroughControls />
<KeyboardHandler />

lines: // because walkthrough.load reads segments.placed which segments.load writes,
// which would put the effect in a self-triggering loop.

input.content:
<script lang="ts">
  import { walkthrough } from '$lib/walkthrough/walkthrough.svelte';

  function handleKeydown(e: KeyboardEvent) {
    switch (e.key) {
      // Within-segment progression
      case 'ArrowDown':
      case ' ':
      case 'Enter':
      case 'PageDown':
        e.preventDefault();
        walkthrough.stepNext();
        break;
      case 'ArrowUp':
      case 'Backspace':
      case 'PageUp':
        e.preventDefault();
        walkthrough.stepPrev();
        break;

      // Cross-segment navigation
      case 'ArrowRight':
        e.preventDefault();
        walkthrough.switchNext();
        break;
      case 'ArrowLeft':
        e.preventDefault();
        walkthrough.switchPrev();
        break;
    }
  }
</script>

<svelte:window onkeydown={handleKeydown} />

<script lang="ts">
  import { walkthrough } from '$lib/walkthrough/walkthrough.svelte';

  function handleKeydown(e: KeyboardEvent) {
    switch (e.key) {
      // Within-segment progression
      case 'ArrowDown':
      case ' ':
      case 'Enter':
      case 'PageDown':
        e.preventDefault();
        walkthrough.stepNext();
        break;
      case 'ArrowUp':
      case 'Backspace':
      case 'PageUp':
        e.preventDefault();
        walkthrough.stepPrev();
        break;

      // Cross-segment navigation
      case 'ArrowRight':
        e.preventDefault();
        walkthrough.switchNext();
        break;
      case 'ArrowLeft':
        e.preventDefault();
        walkthrough.switchPrev();
        break;
    }
  }
</script>

cd "C:/Users/flori/Code/AI for Interactive Media (Presentation)" && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
Two-axis navigation: up/down steps within a segment, left/right switches segments

Per-segment scripts with persistent positions. Up/down (or space) advances
within the current segment; left/right moves between segments. Each segment
remembers where it was left - camera snapshots on leave and restores on return,
stage indices persist on their controllers, so round-tripping preserves state.

- types: add SegmentScript; PresentationConfig now exposes `scripts` instead of
  a flat `steps` list
- walkthrough engine: rewritten around scripts + positions[] + activeIndex.
  stepNext/stepPrev walk a segment's local script with per-segment history for
  undo; switchNext/switchPrev snapshot camera on leave and animate to the
  target segment's saved position (or landing on first visit). Spotlight
  derivation from currentStep still works: it resolves to the active segment's
  current step, so leaving a spotlight clears the highlight, returning restores
  it.
- walkthrough.config: restructured into per-segment scripts. Removes the
  redundant `land-skills`, `to-chat`, and `z20` (back to overview) steps -
  those are now implicit in the cross-segment navigation primitive.
- KeyboardHandler: ArrowDown/Space/Enter -> stepNext, ArrowUp/Backspace ->
  stepPrev, ArrowRight -> switchNext, ArrowLeft -> switchPrev. Page Up/Down
  kept as step aliases for clicker compatibility.
- WalkthroughControls: adds segment prev/next buttons alongside step
  prev/next, with a `seg N / M` indicator next to the step counter.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

content.text:
Done. Committed. Summary of what changed:

**Model**
- `PresentationConfig.scripts: SegmentScript[]` - each segment carries its own local step list.
- Engine tracks `activeIndex` (which segment) and `positions[i]` (last-applied step per segment, -1 = sitting at landing).
- Per-segment camera snapshots + per-segment step history for undo.

**Input**
- `↓` / `Space` / `Enter` / `PgDn` → `stepNext` (within segment)
- `↑` / `Backspace` / `PgUp` → `stepPrev`
- `→` → `switchNext` (to next segment, snapshotting camera on leave, animating to saved position of the new segment)
- `←` → `switchPrev`

**State preservation**
- Chat/skills stage controllers already persisted `stageIndex`; unchanged.
- Camera position snapshotted on every segment leave and animated back on return - so if you were mid-spotlight in chat, switched to skills, then switched back, you land exactly where you left with the spotlight ribbon still lit (because the highlight derivation reads `walkthrough.currentStep` which reflects the restored position).

**Config cleanup**
- Removed the now-redundant `land-skills`, `to-chat`, and final `z20` back-to-overview steps; segment transitions are the `→` key, not script entries.

svelte-check clean. Commit landed as a focused change per the new commit-frequency rule.

<command-name>/logsession</command-name>

content.text:
Append the current session to `docs/session_log.md` and commit concluded session audits.

Steps:
1. Read `docs/session_log.md` to find the last session number and determine the next one.
2. List `session_audits/` (repo root) to find the audit file for this session (match by session ID or most recent file).
3. Review the conversation so far and identify all meaningful contributions from both Human and Claude.
4. Append a new session section to `docs/session_log.md` with:
   - Session heading: `## Session N — YYYY-MM-DD`
   - Audit file link: `**Audit file**: [path](path)` — note that `docs/session_log.md` is one level below the repo root, so audit links must be `../session_audits/...`
   - Contribution table with numbered rows continuing from the last session's numbering
5. Commit the updated `docs/session_log.md`.
6. Commit concluded session audit files (see below).

## Cleaning up stale `.start` markers

Before committing, detect and clean up stale `.start` files — orphans left behind by sessions that crashed or were killed without triggering the cleanup hook.

1. Identify the current session's `.start` file (matches the current audit's 8-char ID).
2. For every OTHER `.start` file, check the modification time of its corresponding `.jsonl` (if one exists):
   - If no `.jsonl` exists at all → **stale** (session never produced output).
   - If the `.jsonl` hasn't been modified in the last 10 minutes → **stale** (session is no longer writing).
   - Otherwise → treat as active (another session may be running concurrently).
3. Delete any stale `.start` files and report which ones were cleaned up.

## Committing concluded session audits

After cleanup, automatically commit any concluded audit files:

1. List all `.jsonl` files in `session_audits/`.
2. For each `.jsonl` file, extract the 8-char session ID from the filename (pattern: `YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM-SS_{id}.jsonl`).
3. Check if a matching `.start` file still exists (pattern: `.{uuid}.start` where the UUID starts with that 8-char ID). If a `.start` file exists, the session is still active — skip it.
4. Stage and commit all concluded (no `.start` file) `.jsonl` files that have uncommitted changes, in a single commit with message: `Log concluded session audits`.
5. Do NOT commit or stage `.start` files, `.gitkeep`, or any active session's `.jsonl`.

## Notes

- If a session audit file hasn't been generated yet (session still active), note the expected filename pattern (`YYYY-MM-DD_HH-MM-SS_{8-char-id}.jsonl`) and leave the link to be confirmed.
- For retrospective logging: if asked to log a past session, read the audit JSONL file to reconstruct contributions.
